sections.heading: Destructuring
sections.body: Extract values from objects and arrays:

code.explanation: Destructuring makes code shorter. Instead of accessing properties one by one, extract them in one line.
Template literals use backticks and ${} for interpolation. Much cleaner than string concatenation.
async/await makes async code look synchronous. Put async before function, await before Promises.

sections.heading: Fetch API
sections.body: The modern way to make HTTP requests:

code.explanation: fetch() returns a Promise. Always check res.ok before parsing JSON.
'...' is the Swiss Army knife of JavaScript. Spread expands, rest collects.

summary: Modern JS: arrow functions, destructuring, template literals, async/await, fetch, spread/rest. These features make code shorter and more readable.
